verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p23) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24 family. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is localized to both the plasma membrane AND Golgi cisternae, confirming dual localization: it cycles between the Golgi/ERGIC and the plasma membrane as part of its vesicular trafficking function. The extracellular/luminal GOLD domain faces outside the cell at the plasma membrane. Its dominant steady-state compartment is the Golgi/ERGIC, making the PM pool a minority, context-dependent fraction rather than its canonical surface residence. This fits 'dual_localization': the protein has its own TM anchor and a documented PM pool alongside a dominant endomembrane compartment. Additionally, as a gamma-secretase complex component, there is documented surface biology in neurons/AD-relevant contexts.
